title: Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari
tagline: Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

heroImage.alt: Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari, Karnataka

overview.short: Tiger and Lion Safari, Tyavarekoppa, Shimoga is located the state of Karnataka, India, with an area of 250 hectares (2.5 km2), at a distance of about 10.0 km (6.2 miles) from Shiva
overview.description: Tiger and Lion Safari, Tyavarekoppa, Shimoga is located the state of Karnataka, India, with an area of 250 hectares (2.5 km2), at a distance of about 10.0 km (6.2 miles) from Shivamogga city, and 275.0 km (170.9 miles) from Bangalore. Started in 1988, it is Karnataka's second safari park, after Bannerghatta National Park near Bangalore. Despite the name, the lion and tiger are neither the only animals here, nor are they the only big cats here.

topPlaces.name: Gadikop
topPlaces.description: Gadikop is a village in Belgaum district in the southern state of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Shimoga
topPlaces.description: Shimoga, officially Shivamogga (Kannada: [ʃiʋɐmoɡːɐ]), is a city, taluka and the district headquarters of Shimoga district in the Karnataka state of India. The city lies on the banks of the Tunga River.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Shimoga
topPlaces.description: The Capture of Shimoga, a town and fortress held by forces of the Kingdom of Mysore, occurred on 3 January 1792, after a preliminary battle with the attacking forces of the British East India Company and the Marathas, not far from the town on 29 December, had scattered much of its defending army. The defenders surrendered after the fort's walls were breached.

topPlaces.name: Adagadi
topPlaces.description: Adagadi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shimoga taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Abbalagere
topPlaces.description: Abbalagere is a village of the Shimoga taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka, India.

items.note: Gadikop is a village in Belgaum district in the southern state of Karnataka, India.

Shimoga, officially Shivamogga (Kannada: [ʃiʋɐmoɡːɐ]), is a city, taluka and the district headquarters of Shimoga district in the Karnataka state of India. The city lies on the banks of the Tunga River.

The Capture of Shimoga, a town and fortress held by forces of the Kingdom of Mysore, occurred on 3 January 1792, after a preliminary battle with the attacking forces of the British East India Company and the Marathas, not far from the town on 29 December, had scattered much of its defending army. The defenders surrendered after the fort's walls were breached.
Adagadi is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Shimoga taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka.
Abbalagere is a village of the Shimoga taluk of Shimoga district in Karnataka, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (130 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (142 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari?
faq.a: Stays in Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tyavarekoppa Lion and Tiger Safari?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gadikop, Shimoga, Battle of Shimoga, Adagadi, Abbalagere.
